text.title: java-spring-async Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for java-spring-async as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Self-invocation: calling this.asyncMethod() inside the same @Service class.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Spring's @Async proxy is not engaged for internal method calls. The method runs synchronously on the caller's thread with no pooling, no error routing, no context propagation. The bug is silent — no exception is thrown, the method just blocks.

failure-mode.description: Bare @Async without a named executor: @Async with no argument or @Async("").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Falls back to SimpleAsyncTaskExecutor, which creates a new OS thread per invocation. Under sustained load, thread count grows without bound until the JVM or OS kills the process. No backpressure, no metrics, no pool reuse.

failure-mode.description: void-returning @Async method with no AsyncUncaughtExceptionHandler registered in AsyncConfig.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Exceptions thrown by void @Async methods are silently discarded. Failures (email send errors, webhook rejections) produce no log, no metric, no alert. Operators have no visibility.

failure-mode.description: @Transactional and @Async on the same method, expecting the caller's transaction to propagate.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The async thread is a different OS thread. Spring cannot propagate a JDBC connection (bound to a ThreadLocal) across threads. The async method reads uncommitted data or opens its own unrelated transaction.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: On JVM shutdown (during deploys), in-flight async tasks are killed immediately. Email sends, webhook deliveries, and audit log writes are silently dropped. Durable work must use a queue, but at minimum the shutdown hook must drain the in-flight pool.

failure-mode.description: Thread.sleep() in tests waiting for async completion.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Produces flaky tests: too short on slow CI, too long on fast hardware. Use Awaitility's await().atMost().untilAsserted() which polls until the condition is met or times out.

failure-mode.description: Using @Async for work that requires durability: charging cards, sending invoices, triggering external state transitions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: @Async tasks live in JVM memory. A JVM restart (crash, OOM, deploy) permanently loses in-flight tasks. Durable work requires a persistent queue (SQS, RabbitMQ, Kafka) with at-least-once delivery.
